purpose: Classifies EM currencies into three structural categories based on energy transition exposure to identify long-term FX trends and trade opportunities.
components: Transition metal exporters (potential winners): CLP, IDR, BRL — benefiting from copper, nickel, lithium demand
Hydrocarbon exporters (secular losers): NGN, COP, EGP — facing declining demand for primary exports
Energy-importing manufacturers (ambiguous): INR, THB, VND — benefiting from lower energy costs but exposed to transition input costs
Novel correlation regime: transition metal prices negatively correlated with energy-importing manufacturer FX
current_assessment: Structural thesis is directionally correct. Early evidence visible (Chile copper revenue rising, Indonesia battery FDI, Nigeria production declining). Not yet investable — proposed trade baskets are effectively concentrated carry trades with transition narratives.
limitations: Timing deeply uncertain — may be 2030+ theme
Institutional quality variation within categories is large
Resource curse 2.0 risk may offset commodity revenue benefits
Concentrated carry trade risk embedded in proposed baskets
